sanskrit:
ज्ञानविज्ञानतृप्तात्मा कूटस्थो विजितेन्द्रियः।
युक्त इत्युच्यते योगी समलोष्टाश्मकाञ्चनः॥
transliteration: jñāna-vijñāna-tṛptātmā kūṭa-stho vijiterdriyaḥ | yukta ity ucyate yogī sama-loṣṭāśma-kāñcanaḥ ||
literal_meaning: One whose self is satisfied with knowledge and realized knowledge, who is steady like an anvil, who has conquered the senses, is said to be ‘yoked’—a yogī who sees clods, stones and gold as equal.
contextual_meaning: True psychological maturity arises when intellectual understanding (jñāna) is metabolized into embodied wisdom (vijñāna), producing an inner satiety that no external object can perturb. The senses lose their grip, affective reactivity flattens, and valuation of objects becomes neutral; gold or gravel activate the same hedonic tone.
psychological_state: Post-gratification homeostasis: the nervous system is no longer pulled toward or away from stimuli; self-worth is internally referenced, producing stable mood and cognitive clarity.
trigger_scenario: After years of chasing promotions and purchases you sit on a park bench, notice a discarded bottle cap and a nearby luxury-car key gleaming in the sun, and feel no difference in visceral pull toward either object.
anchor_text: Let knowledge settle into calm wisdom until stones and gold feel the same.
